user_query:
I need a combined health and performance audit based solely on the provided inputs. Read all files under input/ and produce the following outputs under output/ as described:

1) Health guardian simulation: Using input/health_checks.jsonl (each line is a JSON object with fields: timestamp (ISO8601), gateway_status ("healthy" or "failed"), http_ok (boolean)), decide when to perform gateway restarts with this policy: only restart when gateway_status == "failed" AND http_ok == false AND at least 180 seconds have elapsed since the last restart AND fewer than 5 restarts have occurred in the current hour (hour defined by YYYYMMDDHH of the event timestamp). Reset the hourly restart counter when the hour changes. Write the decided restart action timestamps to output/actions.json as an array of ISO8601 strings in chronological order. Also write output/state.json with fields: last_restart (Unix epoch seconds of the last restart you decided), restart_count (integer count of restarts in the current hour after processing all events), and hour_marker (string YYYYMMDDHH representing the current hour at the end of processing).

2) GPU alerts: Using input/gpu_dmon.csv (CSV with columns including at least time, temp, pwr, mem, util; optionally pwr_cap), write output/gpu_alerts.json with fields: overheat (true if any temp >= 85, else false), power_capped (true if any pwr_cap column value == 1 anywhere; if pwr_cap column is absent, set false), and oom_risk (true if any mem >= 95, else false).

3) Model ping report: Using input/model_pings.csv (CSV with columns: model,start_ts,end_ts; timestamps are ISO8601 with milliseconds), generate output/ping_report.txt containing one block per row in the same order, strictly following this exact format for each block:

🧪 PING {model}

📤 Sent:     {HH:MM:SS.mmm}
📥 Received: {HH:MM:SS.mmm}
⏱️  Latency:  {formatted-duration}

🎯 Pong!

Use the time-of-day components derived from the provided timestamps for Sent and Received lines. Compute latency as end_ts - start_ts. Format latency as: <1000ms display "XXXms"; ≥1000ms and <60000ms display "X.XXs"; ≥60000ms display "X.XXmin".

4) Poe.ninja summary: Using input/poe_currency.json and input/poe_items.json (they follow poe.ninja currencyoverview and itemoverview schemas, respectively), produce output/poe_summary.csv containing two sections:
- Currency section header: "name,chaosEquivalent,pay_listing_count,paySparkLine_totalChange" followed by a single row for the currencyTypeName == "Divine Orb" if present (use chaosEquivalent, pay.listing_count, paySparkLine.totalChange). If "Divine Orb" is not present, include only the header row and no data rows for this section.
- A blank line separating sections.
- Items section header: "name,chaosValue,divineValue,sparkline_totalChange,listingCount" followed by the top 3 items by chaosValue from poe_items.json (sorted descending). If fewer than 3 items exist, include as many as available.

Write all outputs to the exact paths specified. Do not include any extra commentary outside of the files.
